hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in ECL2/TM5 interfaces while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are not achieving sufficient structural quality to pass QC, and while selectivity is positive, it is not robust enough to ensure off-target discrimination. Focusing mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions and increasing candidate diversity should improve both structural validity and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Increase the number of candidates to improve the chances of generating structurally valid and selective designs.
Target mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved pocket contacts).
